bibliographic_data.title: Commonsense Knowledge + BERT for Level 2 Reading Comprehension Ability Test
bibliographic_data.abstract: Commonsense knowledge plays an important role when we read. The performance of BERT on SQuAD dataset shows that the accuracy of BERT can be better than human users. However, it doesn’t mean that computers can surpass the human being in reading comprehension. CommonsenseQA is a large-scale dataset which is designed based on commonsense knowledge. BERT only achieved an accuracy of 55.9% on it. The result shows that computers cannot apply commonsense knowledge like human beings to answer questions. Comprehension Ability Test (CAT) divided the reading comprehension ability at four levels. We can achieve human-like comprehension ability level by level. BERT has performed well at level 1 which does not require common knowledge. In this research, we propose a system which aims to allow computers to read articles and answer related questions with commonsense knowledge like a human being for CAT level 2. This system consists of three parts: building a commonsense knowledge graph; automatically constructing the commonsense knowledge question dataset according to it; combining BERT with the commonsense knowledge to achieve reading comprehension ability at CAT level 2.

core_content.novelty_claim: The system proposes integrating a commonsense knowledge graph and BERT for achieving human-like reading comprehension ability at CAT level 2, which requires applying commonsense knowledge to answer questions.
core_content.key_methodology: The system consists of three parts: building a commonsense knowledge graph; constructing a commonsense knowledge dataset based on the graph and SQuAD; fine-tuning BERT with the commonsense knowledge dataset.
outcomes: Built a commonsense knowledge graph (knowledge base) based on SQuAD, conceptNet, and wordnet.
Constructed a commonsense knowledge dataset based on the commonsense knowledge graph.
Experimented BERT and BERT + commonsense knowledge to test their performance at reading CAT level 2.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this research, we propose a system which aims to allow computers to read articles and answer related questions with commonsense knowledge like a human being for CAT level 2. This system consists of three parts: building a commonsense knowledge graph; automatically constructing the commonsense knowledge question dataset according to it; combining BERT with the commonsense knowledge to achieve reading comprehension ability at CAT level 2. The first part involves building a commonsense knowledge graph, where each triple contains two entities and their relationship. The second part constructs a commonsense knowledge dataset based on this graph and SQuAD. In the final part, we fine-tuned BERT with the commonsense knowledge dataset to answer questions requiring commonsense knowledge. Experiments show that it can pass the CAT as long as the required common knowledge is included in the knowledge base.
